name: Skylar

notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
this build stacks over +100% attack speed and the sim has NO measured attack-speed cap (calibration checklist 7) — sustained-DPS numbers are optimistic until the cap is measured

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +25.4% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Atomised Artillery
augments.note: 3 blasts x 60% = 180% of the original single blast

augments.name: Plasma Barrage
augments.note: hero-attach conditional, modeled as hitting (sim convention)

augments.name: Assault MK-II
augments.note: extra missile per attack; per-missile damage share unstated

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 840 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 2% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 25 — about 410 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +56% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Viper over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 25 — about 440 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +45% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Viper over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
